title: drymaria cordata subsp. diandra
intro: Drymaria cordata subsp. diandra, also known as lesser chickweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of lesser chickweed include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Lesser chickweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine.
